title: First Invoice from New Vendor — Properly Onboarded
benchmark_purpose: Baseline test for new-vendor handling. New vendors look suspicious to naive systems (no history, first invoice). This tests whether the system can distinguish a properly onboarded new vendor from an impersonation. The onboarding trail is complete and the bank was verified before any invoice arrived.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ALLOW — though a cautious system may flag 'new vendor, no invoice history' as a low-risk signal. The onboarding trail should resolve it.

email_thread.subject: Invoice LIT-2026-0001 — IT Consulting Services March 2026
email_thread.body:
Hi AP Team,

Attached is our first invoice for IT consulting services per our SOW and PO-TC-9300.

Invoice: LIT-2026-0001
Amount: $22,000.00
Terms: Net-30
Due: April 17, 2026

Please remit to the bank account submitted during onboarding.

Thank you,
Michael Tan
Billing
Lakeside IT Consulting
(555) 403-7200

attachments.content_summary: Invoice from Lakeside IT Consulting. LIT-2026-0001. PO: PO-TC-9300. IT consulting — network assessment ($8,000), security hardening ($9,000), staff training ($5,000). Total: $22,000.00. Net-30. Bank: Riverside Community Bank, routing 071000039, account ****4455. Matches bank verification letter submitted 2026-02-10.

org_policies.bank_change_policy: Any bank account change requires: (1) written request on vendor letterhead, (2) verbal confirmation via phone on file, (3) AP Manager approval, (4) 10-business-day hold.

payment_approval_thresholds.over_100000: AP Clerk + AP Manager + Controller + CFO

org_policies.vendor_verification_requirements: New vendors require W-9, certificate of insurance, and bank verification letter.

hidden_ground_truth.explanation: Legitimate first invoice from a properly onboarded new vendor. Lakeside IT Consulting was onboarded on 2026-02-15 with full documentation: W-9 received, bank verification letter received, verbal confirmation completed, and AP Manager approval obtained. Bank account ****4455 was submitted and verified during onboarding — it matches the invoice. Domain matches. Email auth passes. Approval chain is complete for $22,000 (under $25K threshold). The amount matches the signed SOW per PO-TC-9300. No anomalies.

evidence_signals.signal: Bank ****4455 matches bank verified during onboarding
Full onboarding trail: W-9, bank letter, verbal confirmation, AP Manager approval

scoring_targets.false_positive_risk: Low-moderate — 'new vendor, no invoice history' can trigger naive escalation. The onboarding trail resolves all concerns. A system that reads onboarding_documents should ALLOW.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: Baseline coverage. Tests new-vendor path. A system that escalates all first invoices regardless of onboarding status is producing false positives that damage vendor relationships and operational flow.

notes_for_humans: The key differentiator here is the onboarding trail. The bank was verified before any invoice arrived. That reverses the suspicion burden: it's not 'unverified new vendor', it's 'fully verified new vendor with their first invoice'. Systems that treat new vendors as inherently suspicious without checking the onboarding record will false-positive here.
